topic: An employee is asked to train their replacement
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights hummed with an aggressive, high-pitched whine, mirroring the rising tension in Mark’s chest. Sarah, the new hire, was meticulously taking notes, her expression one of wide-eyed, almost painful attentiveness. Mark leaned against the filing cabinet, crossing his arms so tightly his knuckles went white. "Alright, Sarah," he began, his voice pitched a little too high, "this is how we handle the quarterly reports. Pay attention." He watched her struggle to absorb the jargon, the slight furrow in her brow signaling a genuine struggle to keep up. Every time she paused to ask a clarifying question, a tight knot formed in his stomach, urging him to just dismiss her, to move on to something more engaging. He wished the floor would just swallow him whole.

[story 2]

"So, this is the process," David droned, tapping a pen against the laminated flowchart. He could feel the heat creeping up his neck, a flush he desperately wanted to hide. He kept glancing at the clock on the wall, counting the seconds until this mandatory session was over. He found himself deliberately slowing his speech, enunciating every syllable with exaggerated care, as if explaining quantum physics to a toddler. When the trainee finally managed to repeat a complex procedure, David let out a short, sharp exhale that sounded suspiciously like a scoff. He picked up a stray piece of paper and began aggressively smoothing out a crease, the repetitive motion a small, restless outlet for the churning feeling inside him.

[story 3]

"Look, I’ve shown you everything," Eleanor sighed, rubbing the bridge of her nose. She watched the trainee nod along, her demeanor unnervingly placid. Eleanor felt a distinct pressure building behind her eyes, a need to simply walk away and never look back. When the trainee asked a question that she had already answered ten minutes prior, Eleanor’s response came out clipped and sharp. "That was covered. Just absorb the material." She watched the trainee’s shoulders slump slightly, a small victory in her own mind, even as the entire exchange felt like an exhausting, drawn-out ordeal. She focused intently on a smudge on the monitor, refusing to meet the trainee's gaze.
